article.title: NVIDIA Reports Record $81.6 Billion Q1 Revenue as AI Factory Buildout Drives 85 Percent Annual Growth
article.summary: NVIDIA's Q1 FY2027 data center revenue hit $75.2 billion, up 92% year-over-year, as Blackwell ramps at record speed and Vera Rubin production is set for Q3 2026.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

NVIDIA reported record first-quarter fiscal 2027 revenue of $81.6 billion on May 20, 2026, an 85 percent increase from a year earlier and a 20 percent sequential gain, [according to the company's earnings release](https://www.stocktitan.net/news/NVDA/nvidia-announces-financial-results-for-first-quarter-fiscal-fq78amc9h84m.html). The result cleared Wall Street consensus estimates of approximately $79.2 billion [by more than $2 billion](https://www.tradingkey.com/analysis/stocks/us-stocks/261918835-nvidia-q1-fy2027-earnings-breakdown-tradingkey). CEO Jensen Huang characterized demand as having "gone parabolic" as agentic AI workloads joined large-scale model training in driving the company's expansion, [according to the earnings call transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2026/05/20/nvidia-nvda-q1-2027-earnings-transcript/).

## Financial Results

Data center revenue, now the dominant segment, reached $75.2 billion in Q1, a 92 percent year-over-year increase and a 21 percent sequential gain, [per the company's SEC filing](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/NVDA/8-k-nvidia-corp-reports-material-event-56086a88bbb4.html). Within the data center, the hyperscale subsegment — covering cloud providers' own infrastructure — generated $37.9 billion, up 115 percent from the year-ago quarter. The AI clouds, industrial, and enterprise subsegment added $37.4 billion, up 31 percent sequentially.

Data center compute revenue totaled $60.4 billion, while networking — driven by the Spectrum-X ethernet platform that ships alongside Blackwell systems — reached $14.8 billion, a nearly threefold increase year-over-year, [according to KuCoin's breakdown](https://www.kucoin.com/blog/nvidia-q1-fy-2027-earnings-record-revenue-and-$91-billion-guidance-crush-wall-street-estimates).

The edge computing platform, which encompasses gaming PCs, workstations, robotics, and automotive devices under a new reporting structure NVIDIA adopted this quarter, contributed $6.4 billion, up 29 percent from the prior year, [per the SEC filing](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/NVDA/8-k-nvidia-corp-reports-material-event-56086a88bbb4.html).

GAAP gross margin was 74.9 percent, up from 60.5 percent in the year-ago quarter. GAAP net income reached $58.3 billion, compared with $18.8 billion a year earlier, though the GAAP figure was boosted by $15.9 billion in unrealized gains on the company's equity investment portfolio, [according to TradingKey](https://www.tradingkey.com/analysis/stocks/us-stocks/261918835-nvidia-q1-fy2027-earnings-breakdown-tradingkey). Non-GAAP diluted earnings per share were $1.87, beating the analyst consensus of approximately $1.78, [per the earnings release](https://www.stocktitan.net/news/NVDA/nvidia-announces-financial-results-for-first-quarter-fiscal-fq78amc9h84m.html). Operating cash flow totaled $50.3 billion and free cash flow reached $48.6 billion, [per the SEC filing](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/NVDA/8-k-nvidia-corp-reports-material-event-56086a88bbb4.html).

## Capital Returns and Q2 Guidance

NVIDIA's board approved an increase in the quarterly dividend from $0.01 to $0.25 per share and authorized an additional $80.0 billion in share repurchases, with no stated expiration, [according to the earnings release](https://www.stocktitan.net/news/NVDA/nvidia-announces-financial-results-for-first-quarter-fiscal-fq78amc9h84m.html). The company returned approximately $20.0 billion to shareholders during the quarter through buybacks and dividends.

For the second quarter of fiscal 2027, NVIDIA guided for revenue of $91.0 billion, plus or minus 2 percent, with GAAP gross margin of 74.9 percent, [per the SEC filing](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/NVDA/8-k-nvidia-corp-reports-material-event-56086a88bbb4.html). The Q2 guidance itself exceeded analyst consensus by approximately $3.8 billion, [according to TradingKey](https://www.tradingkey.com/analysis/stocks/us-stocks/261918835-nvidia-q1-fy2027-earnings-breakdown-tradingkey). Despite the broad beat across revenue, earnings, and forward guidance, the stock moved little in after-hours trading, closing nearly flat, as the market has come to expect consistent outperformance from the company.

## Blackwell and the Road to Vera Rubin

The quarter was the first full reporting period since NVIDIA began shipping its Blackwell-architecture AI accelerators at scale. Jensen Huang described the ramp as "the fastest product ramp in our company's history," [per the Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2026/05/20/nvidia-nvda-q1-2027-earnings-transcript/). The Blackwell Ultra variant, which began shipping in early 2026, delivered a 2.7x increase in throughput and a 60 percent reduction in cost per token versus its predecessor, [according to Beta Finch](https://betafinch.com/episodes/NVDA_2027_Q1_en).

The next-generation Vera Rubin platform, [first detailed by The Machine Herald in February](/article/2026-02/04-nvidia-unveils-rubin-a-six-chip-platform-promising-10x-cost-reduction-for-ai-inference), is scheduled to begin production shipments in Q3 2026, [per the earnings call transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2026/05/20/nvidia-nvda-q1-2027-earnings-transcript/). Vera Rubin claims 3.5 times the training performance and 5 times the inference performance of Blackwell, with inference costs reduced to one-seventh, [according to Tech Times](https://www.techtimes.com/articles/317079/20260524/nvidia-computex-2026-jensen-huang-flies-tsmc-vera-rubin-ramp-strains-taiwan-supply-chain.htm). The system's six-chip architecture — combining the Vera CPU, Rubin GPU, NVLink 6 switch, ConnectX-9 SuperNIC, BlueField-4 DPU, and Spectrum-X ethernet switch — requires approximately 150 Taiwanese supply-chain partners and is what Huang called "the largest product launch, probably in the history of Taiwan," [Tech Times reported](https://www.techtimes.com/articles/317079/20260524/nvidia-computex-2026-jensen-huang-flies-tsmc-vera-rubin-ramp-strains-taiwan-supply-chain.htm).

Huang traveled to Taipei on May 23, arriving at Songshan Airport to meet TSMC Chairman C.C. Wei ahead of Computex 2026, with the primary agenda being production commitments for Vera Rubin, [according to Tech Times](https://www.techtimes.com/articles/317079/20260524/nvidia-computex-2026-jensen-huang-flies-tsmc-vera-rubin-ramp-strains-taiwan-supply-chain.htm). TSMC is scaling its CoWoS advanced packaging capacity from roughly 35,000 wafers per month in late 2024 toward 120,000 to 140,000 wafers per month by the end of 2026, a nearly fourfold increase, and NVIDIA has pre-committed more than half of TSMC's available CoWoS capacity through 2027. Huang noted that the Vera Rubin ramp overlaps with the tail end of Grace Blackwell GB300 production, describing the second half of 2026 as "a very busy second half" for Taiwan's supply chain.

## The Vera CPU Opportunity

Alongside the GPU business, NVIDIA is making its first major push into the CPU market with the Vera processor, [as The Machine Herald reported when the company began delivering initial units to Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceXAI](/article/2026-05/21-nvidia-hand-delivers-first-vera-cpus-to-anthropic-openai-and-spacexai-as-agentic-ai-drives-a-new-cpu-moment). Huang told analysts that Vera "opens a $200 billion TAM for NVIDIA, a market we've never addressed before," [per the Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2026/05/20/nvidia-nvda-q1-2027-earnings-transcript/). The company projected approximately $20 billion in total CPU revenue for the current fiscal year, [according to Beta Finch](https://betafinch.com/episodes/NVDA_2027_Q1_en).

## China Revenue Effectively Zero

CFO Colette Kress stated plainly on the call that the company is "not including any China data center compute revenue" in its outlook, [per the Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2026/05/20/nvidia-nvda-q1-2027-earnings-transcript/). In practice, the company's share of China's AI chip market has declined from approximately 95 percent to effectively zero, [according to Tech Times](https://www.techtimes.com/articles/317079/20260524/nvidia-computex-2026-jensen-huang-flies-tsmc-vera-rubin-ramp-strains-taiwan-supply-chain.htm), as Huawei's Ascend 950PR entered mass production in March 2026 and the Chinese government has directed domestic buyers away from US hardware. The H200 export licenses the US government approved for sales to approximately ten Chinese firms — including Alibaba, Tencent, and ByteDance, [as The Machine Herald reported in May](/article/2026-05/16-us-clears-nvidia-h200-sales-to-ten-chinese-firms-with-25-revenue-cut-but-beijing-tells-buyers-to-stand-down) — have resulted in zero completed deliveries as of late May 2026. Huawei is projected to generate $12 billion in AI chip revenue in 2026, a 60 percent increase from $7.5 billion in 2025, with approximately 750,000 units planned for shipment, [Tech Times reported](https://www.techtimes.com/articles/317079/20260524/nvidia-computex-2026-jensen-huang-flies-tsmc-vera-rubin-ramp-strains-taiwan-supply-chain.htm).

Huang pushed back on the status quo on the earnings call, saying that "conceding an entire market the size of China probably does not make a lot of strategic sense" and calling for more dynamic policy, [per the Motley Fool transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2026/05/20/nvidia-nvda-q1-2027-earnings-transcript/).

## What We Don't Know

NVIDIA did not disclose gaming revenue as a standalone figure under its new reporting structure, which consolidates gaming, professional visualization, robotics, and automotive into the edge computing platform. The company also did not provide unit shipment volumes for Blackwell chips. Inventory on the balance sheet surged $4.4 billion to $25.8 billion in a single quarter — a 20 percent increase — which [TradingKey noted](https://www.tradingkey.com/analysis/stocks/us-stocks/261918835-nvidia-q1-fy2027-earnings-breakdown-tradingkey) represents a potential impairment risk if enterprise capital expenditure moderates or export controls expand further. The $73.6 billion equity investment portfolio, up 95 percent quarter-over-quarter, contributed $15.9 billion in unrealized gains to GAAP net income this quarter, making the headline profit figure sensitive to market sentiment shifts. Whether Vera Rubin production will meet the Q3 2026 target in the face of TSMC's constrained CoWoS capacity remains an open question.
